body.title: Migrate wg_edges words into memories as WORD_GRAPH nodes
body.result: 57,298 words from wg_edges migrated to clan.memories as WORD_GRAPH nodes; links_weighted populated from wg_edges similarity weights; m061 unique index; get_or_create_word_memory() in cortex; WORD_GRAPH excluded from search
body.intention: I intend that word-graph words are first-class memory nodes sharing the stable node ID scheme, calving infrastructure, and spreading activation pipeline.
body.description:
wg_edges holds 57K distinct source words (semantic similarity graph, ~20 edges/word) that are not yet memory nodes. Migrate them into clan.memories as WORD_GRAPH type, with co-occurrence edges stored in links_weighted using the existing semantic weights from wg_edges. No new embedding calls needed for v1 — wg_edges similarity weights port directly.

Scope: 57K words × 20 edges avg = ~1.1M links_weighted entries. Batched background migration (1000 words/tx).

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/cortex.py (add WORD_GRAPH memory_type, add get_or_create_word_memory() helper, batched migration), lab/claudecode/migrate_wg_to_memories.py (one-time migration script, idempotent)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; append-only (no DELETE); batch writes 1000/tx; WORD_GRAPH memory_type added; links_weighted format {memory_id: similarity_weight} sourced from wg_edges (semantic), NOT wg_cooccur (frequency) — preserves existing spread_from_words semantics; stable node IDs via node_id.generate()
**Scope boundary:** IN: word→memory migration, wg_edges weights→links_weighted (top-100 per word), get_or_create_word_memory() helper. OUT: embedding vectors on word nodes (separate decision — model choice not made), wg_cooccur/wg_edges table removal (T-wg-cooccur-retire), spreading activation update (T-wg-spread-via-cortex)
**Completion criteria:** SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memories WHERE memory_type='WORD_GRAPH' >= 50000 after migration; 5 spot-check words have stable node IDs, correct links_weighted entries sourced from wg_edges; migration script is idempotent (run twice = same result, no duplicates); existing igor test suite passes.
